text: More importantly, counter-hegemonic narratives are not only about uncovering marginalized histories; they are also about preventing the formation of new memories tied to old narratives. We mention the case of monuments and their destruction to illustrate this point. Activists around the world have destroyed monuments in order to challenge dominant representations of historical events. However, as we shall argue, monuments not only offend but also constitute a space for protesting and therefore for practicing the art of dissidence. Therefore, it is crucial to discuss possible ways in which we can challenge current representations through existing monuments and add counter-narratives to those already present in the public space.
